metadata.title:
Mann COMES
TO the AID


Of POLICE

GAYS THEY WERE EVER GEN.
TIEMEN IN DEALING WITH


WOMEN PRISONERS.

pages.raw_text:
A typewriter statement eight
pages in length has been issued by
Police Justice s. A. Mann as de-
Tense of his action in handling the
j. w. w. cases. Judge Mann aspires
to represent this district in con-
gress and for fear that the voters
over the district might misinterpret
his attitude in dealing with the
street speaking cases he goes into
some details to explain.


Judge Mann's version of the
street speaking controversy does
not tally with the official statement
prepared by the Central Labor
council The judge defends the po-
lice and other city authorities, his
own decisions, and says in conclu-
sion:


Il have been associated with the
police department Of Spokane in
various ways during the last six
years and the first report has yet
to come to me in which charge of
ungentlemanly conduct toward
women is made against the officers:
/ believe that the statements and
charges made by women associ-
ated with the l. w. w. are absolute
~ untrue, and are made solely for
the purpose of notoriety, and at
the same time casting reflections
upon the police department of this
city, to gain the sympathy of the
good residents of this country"


This last statement is in direct
conflict with the claim of the mem
bers of the Woman's club leading
in the fight for matrons at the city
Jail. If the women had the ballot
Judge Mann might be placed in
the peculiar position of being called
on the carpet and made to explain.
The women have sworn statements
in contradiction of this conclusion
of Judge Mann as to the purity OF
the police department.
